qtype: order of operations and calculator use
tex:
Edelgard tries to calculate

\[\dfrac{68 + 18}{9 - 5}\]

(a) She types into her calculator $68 + 18 \div 9 - 5$.
\newline
Explain why this does not give Edelgard the correct answer.
\newline

(b) Work out the correct answer to

\[\dfrac{68 + 18}{9 - 5}\]

content.content:
She types into her calculator $68 + 18 \div 9 - 5$.
\newline
Explain why this does not give Edelgard the correct answer.
\newline

Work out the correct answer to

\[\dfrac{68 + 18}{9 - 5}\]

qtype: compound interest and percentage increase comparison
tex:
Sonia wants to invest \$5000 for 6 years.
\newline
Bank A pays compound interest at a rate of 3.5\% per year.
\newline
Bank B increases the \$5000 by 22\% at the end of 6 years.
\newline
Which bank will give Sonia the most money at the end of 6 years and by how much?
You must show all your working.
\newline

Bank  will give \$  more money.
\newline

stem.content:
Sonia wants to invest \$5000 for 6 years.
\newline
Bank A pays compound interest at a rate of 3.5\% per year.
\newline
Bank B increases the \$5000 by 22\% at the end of 6 years.
\newline
Which bank will give Sonia the most money at the end of 6 years and by how much?
You must show all your working.
\newline

Bank \AnswerLine[][] will give \$ \AnswerLine[][] more money.
\newline

_rawBody:
Sonia wants to invest \$5000 for 6 years.
Bank A pays compound interest at a rate of 3.5\% per year.
Bank B increases the \$5000 by 22\% at the end of 6 years.
Which bank will give Sonia the most money at the end of 6 years and by how much?
You must show all your working.

Bank \AnswerLine[][] will give \$ \AnswerLine[][] more money.
\Marks{5}
